warnings: mode=adiabatic: each oxidation state was geometry-optimized separately and the potential uses ΔE between the relaxed geometries (includes reorganization). It does NOT include ZPE/thermal/entropy (ΔE used in place of ΔG); use mode=freq for a true ΔG.

Solvation is implicit/continuum only (or gas phase) — no explicit solvent shell and no Born–Haber solvation cycle. Semi-empirical redox potentials are screening-grade (≈±0.3–0.5 V); even DFT here omits the explicit-solvation correction. Treat as a screening estimate.

E°(vs SHE) uses E_abs(SHE) = 4.281 V. The absolute SHE potential is convention-dependent (literature ≈4.28–4.44 V); this is a systematic offset on the reported potential. Override with --e-abs-she to match your reference convention.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Standard redox potential (0 → −1 couple) for naphthoquinone — GFN2-xTB in implicit water (ALPB), referenced to the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE).

Method provenance:
1. Property: Standard redox potential, E° (V vs SHE), adiabatic ΔE (no vibrational/thermal correction).
2. Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding).
3. Geometry provenance: Each oxidation state (oxidized charge 0 multiplicity 1; reduced charge −1 multiplicity 2) was independently geometry-optimized at GFN2-xTB (xtb/ASE BFGS optimizer, default fmax 0.05 eV Å⁻¹). Both optimizations converged.
4. Environment: implicit water (ALPB continuum); no explicit solvent.
5. Charge/multiplicity: oxidized 0/1; reduced −1/2.
6. Software: chemkit redox-potential skill (xtb backend v. as bundled), live log at
   /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/redox-potential-validation/naphthoquinone/20260625-115519_naphthoquinone_redox_potential/redox_20260625-115529.out (written during run).
7. Convergence & caveats: Both optimizations converged; see warnings echoed above. Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB. Single-conformer, no ZPE/thermal corrections; screening-grade accuracy (~0.3–0.5 V).

Headline result (from chemkit JSON key redox_potential_V_vs_SHE): 4.5884454 V vs SHE.

All warnings from the engine are reproduced verbatim above. The calculation passed chemkit’s integrity checks (integrity.status: ok; trustworthy: true).
